On March 14 and March 24, 2016, the advisory committee met to discuss and consider the engagement of a financial advisor, as authorized at the March 10, 2016 Board meeting.
The advisory committee authorized Company management to inquire whether Barclays would be available to act as a financial advisor to the Company in connection with a potential strategic transaction.
April 19, 2016, the Company countersigned an engagement letter with Barclays, dated April 15, 2016, as authorized during the April 15, 2016 Board meeting.
Of the parties contacted, three strategic parties and four financial sponsors executed confidentiality agreements with the Company, including Thoma Bravo on May 10, 2016.

Barclays reported to the Board that at such time ten of the 11 strategic parties contacted by Barclays had declined to participate in the strategic process, and that while Strategic 3 had executed a confidentiality agreement and met with management, it did not submit a bid by the June 9, 2016 deadline.

cohorts.label: Confidentiality-agreement signers
cohorts.membership_basis: Parties that executed confidentiality agreements with the Company during the strategic process.

cohorts.label: Initial IOI recipients
cohorts.membership_basis: Parties that had executed confidentiality agreements and attended meetings with Company management by June 3, 2016.

cohorts.label: Second-phase bidders
cohorts.membership_basis: Parties advanced to the second phase of the strategic process after the June 9, 2016 indications of interest.

cohorts.label: Four financial sponsors with confidentiality agreements
cohorts.membership_basis: Financial sponsors that executed confidentiality agreements with the Company during the May 6 through June 9, 2016 outreach period.

date.raw_text: In early 2015, and again in June 2015
date.resolution_note: Captures two informal approaches described in one sentence.

observations.summary: In early 2015 and again in June 2015, Thoma Bravo informally approached the Company and expressed interest in a possible business transaction without making a specific proposal.
observations.other_detail: No specific proposals were made during these meetings.

observations.summary: In January 2016, Thoma Bravo reiterated its interest in a potential transaction and made no specific proposal.
Thoma Bravo sent the Board an unsolicited, non-binding indication of interest to acquire the Company for $15.00 per share in cash.
The Board began evaluating Thoma Bravo's indication of interest and authorized an advisory committee to recommend a financial advisor.
observations.other_detail: Board evaluation of Thoma Bravo's indication of interest and authorization of an advisory committee to recommend a financial advisor.

observations.summary: The advisory committee met on March 14 and March 24, 2016 to consider engaging a financial advisor and authorized management to inquire whether Barclays would be available to act for the Company in connection with a potential strategic transaction.
The Board engaged Barclays as the Company's financial advisor.
The Company countersigned Barclays' engagement letter, dated April 15, 2016, as authorized during the April 15, 2016 Board meeting.
The Board decided to further explore a potential business combination transaction and approved confidential outreach to the indicative list of parties.

date.raw_text: During the period from May 6 through June 9, 2016

observations.summary: During the May 6 through June 9, 2016 outreach period, four financial sponsors executed confidentiality agreements with the Company, including Thoma Bravo on May 10, 2016.

date.raw_text: During the period from May 6 through June 9, 2016

observations.summary: Three strategic parties and four financial sponsors executed confidentiality agreements with the Company during the strategic process.
Barclays sent bid instruction letters on behalf of the Company to six parties requesting indications of interest by June 9, 2016.
attachments: subset of the financial information contained in the Company's long-range plan
observations.other_detail: Requested per-share offer price, financing sources, future plans, diligence needs, and transaction structure.

observations.summary: Strategic 1 said it was no longer interested in the strategic process and would not submit an indication of interest.

date.resolution_note: The filing states only that Strategic 3 had executed the confidentiality agreement by the June 9, 2016 deadline.

observations.summary: By the June 9, 2016 deadline, Strategic 3 had executed a confidentiality agreement with the Company and met with management.
Sponsor A submitted a written preliminary non-binding indication of interest at $16.50 per share.
Sponsor B submitted a written preliminary non-binding indication of interest at $17.00 to $18.00 per share.
Thoma Bravo submitted a written preliminary non-binding indication of interest at $17.25 per share and provided a draft merger agreement.
The Board advanced Sponsor A, Sponsor B, and Thoma Bravo to the second phase of the strategic process.
Strategic 2 said it was no longer interested in exploring a potential transaction.
Strategic 3 said it was no longer interested in exploring a potential transaction.
Sponsor A said any second-round bid would not be meaningfully higher than its June 9 indication of interest.
The Board formed the Special Committee to oversee the strategic process.
observations.other_detail: Formation of the Special Committee of independent and disinterested directors to oversee the strategic process.

observations.summary: The Special Committee said it was not interested in pursuing Sponsor A at its initial-bid price.
Strategic 4 said an acquisition of the Company was not a strategic fit and that it was not interested in participating in the strategic process.
Barclays sent final bid process letters to Sponsor B and Thoma Bravo requesting marked drafts and setting a July 8 final bid deadline.
observations.other_detail: Final bid process letters requested marked drafts and final bids.

observations.summary: Sponsor B said any final bid would be significantly below its June 9 indication of interest.
The Special Committee decided not to pursue Sponsor B at a price significantly below its original indication.
Thoma Bravo, through Kirkland, sent a revised draft of the merger agreement to Goodwin.
observations.other_detail: Kirkland sent Goodwin a revised draft of the merger agreement.

observations.summary: Thoma Bravo submitted the only final bid at $19.00 per share in cash and said it was prepared to execute its revised draft merger agreement.
Thoma Bravo submitted a revised written non-binding best-and-final proposal at $19.25 per share.
Barclays informed Thoma Bravo that it should submit its best and final offer.
Kirkland sent Goodwin a draft exclusivity agreement, but the Company declined to enter into it.
The Board approved the merger agreement with Thoma Bravo and recommended stockholder approval.
observations.other_detail: Board approval of the merger agreement and recommendation to stockholders.

observations.summary: The Special Committee unanimously recommended that the Board enter into the merger agreement with Thoma Bravo.
observations.other_detail: Special Committee recommendation that the Board enter into the merger agreement with Thoma Bravo.

observations.summary: The parties finalized and executed the merger agreement.
The parties executed the merger agreement.
The Company and Thoma Bravo issued a joint press release announcing execution of the merger agreement.
